medications.name: Metformin
medications.frequency: twice a day, with breakfast and dinner
medications.purpose: helps control your blood sugar levels
medications.special_instructions: take with food to avoid an upset stomach

medications.name: Lisinopril
medications.purpose: keeps your blood pressure at a safe level
medications.special_instructions: avoid potassium supplements while taking this

medications.name: Warfarin
medications.frequency: once every evening at the same time
medications.purpose: prevents blood clots from forming after your surgery
medications.special_instructions: do not take with ibuprofen or aspirin; you will need a blood test (INR) in 5 days

medications.name: Omeprazole
medications.frequency: once in the morning, 30 minutes before eating
medications.purpose: protects your stomach lining while you recover

activity_restrictions: no lifting anything heavier than 10 pounds for 4 weeks
no driving for at least 2 weeks or while taking prescription pain medication
short walks around the house are encouraged starting the day after discharge
no swimming or submerging the incision site until cleared by your doctor
return to normal activity gradually — stop if you feel pain or dizziness
wound_care: keep the incision sites clean and dry for the first 48 hours
after 48 hours, gently wash with mild soap and water, then pat dry
cover with a clean bandage daily until the skin is fully closed
do not apply creams, lotions, or ointments unless instructed by Dr. Patel
steri-strips (small white tape strips) will fall off on their own — do not pull them

follow_ups.purpose: post-surgery wound check and medication review
INR blood test to check Warfarin levels

warning_signs: redness, swelling, or discharge from the incision site
severe abdominal pain that is not relieved by your pain medication
yellowing of the skin or eyes (jaundice)
unusual bruising or bleeding that does not stop
shortness of breath or chest pain — call 911 immediately
